prs.title: feat: customizable quiz start page content (quiz_header)
prs.body:
Admins can now set the "Why should you take this quiz?" text per quiz instead of showing a hardcoded paragraph. The field is edited via a rich-text editor in       
  QuizBuilder and rendered on the quiz start screen before the user clicks Start.
                                                                                                                                                                      
  Changes:        
  - add_quiz_header_column.sql — SQLite migration adding nullable quiz_header TEXT column to quizzes
  - server-auth-test.js — POST /api/quizzes and PUT /api/quizzes/:id now read/persist quiz_header                                                                     
  - QuizBuilder.jsx — new "Quiz Start Page Content" rich-text field; wired into create, update, edit-load, and cancel flows
  - QuizAttempt.jsx — start screen renders quiz.quiz_header as HTML when set; falls back to original hardcoded text for quizzes that haven't been updated             
  (non-breaking)

prs.title: Enable Canvas Drag & Select Modes #744
prs.body:
This PR implements cursor-based canvas dragging functionality with support for two distinct interaction modes:

Select Mode:
The canvas remains static, allowing users to interact with and select individual elements without any unintended movement.
Drag Mode:
The entire canvas can be smoothly dragged and repositioned using the cursor, providing better navigation for large diagrams.

This update improves the overall usability and user experience by clearly separating interaction and navigation behaviors. The implementation ensures smooth mode switching, accurate cursor tracking, and prevents conflicts between dragging and element selection.

I worked on issue #744
@1ilit

This pull request focuses on improving the Footer section’s user interface by refining its overall layout, spacing, and visual hierarchy.

By enhancing the spacing between elements and adjusting their alignment, the footer becomes easier to navigate and visually more balanced. issues.title: [FEATURE] Sidebar Table Grouping for Easier Navigation

labels.name: duplicate
labels.description: This issue or pull request already exists

issues.body:
Is your feature request tied to a challenge? Please explain.
When a diagram contains many tables, the list on the left sidebar becomes overwhelming and difficult to browse. Finding particular tables quickly is inconvenient, which slows down productivity, especially when handling large or complex databases.

Proposed solution
Introduce a grouping option for the table list on the left side. Suggested capabilities include:

Letting users build custom groups (for example, by business domain, functionality, or any logical classification)

Supporting drag-and-drop so tables can be reassigned between groups

Allowing groups to expand or collapse, so only relevant tables are visible, reducing clutter

Extra details
This improvement would greatly enhance the tool’s usability for database-heavy projects. Organizing tables into meaningful structures would make the interface more user-friendly and manageable, particularly in projects with a large number of tables.

@1ilit

issues.title: Profile data retention
issues.body:
We retain user profile data to provide a seamless and personalized experience across our platform. This includes information such as name, contact details, preferences, activity history, and other relevant metadata. Profile data is securely stored and maintained only for as long as necessary to fulfill the intended purposes, such as account management, service improvement, and compliance with legal obligations. Users may request access, modification, or deletion of their profile data at any time in accordance with our privacy policy.
Code according to this

issues.title: Make "Forgot password" functioning
issues.body:
The "Forgot Password" feature is currently not functioning. When users click the "Forgot Password" link, no password reset email is sent, or the flow does not guide them through the password recovery process. This prevents users from regaining access to their accounts if they forget their login credentials.



Expected Behavior:
When a user clicks "Forgot Password" and enters their email address, the system should send a password reset email to the provided address with instructions to reset their password.

issues.title: Make a form under Hackathon - Create team button

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issues.body:
Under the Hackathon section, implement a "Create Team" button that, when clicked, displays a form allowing users to register a new team for the event.

The form should include fields such as:

-Team Name

-Team Members' Names or Emails

-Project Idea (optional)

This feature enables participants to easily form and register teams directly from the Hackathon page. The form can appear as a popup modal or expand inline for a smooth and user-friendly experience. Once submitted, the team information should be stored or forwarded to the backend for registration and further processing.
